intro: What lazysite does, grouped by what you are trying to achieve. Each capability links to its 'how it works' reference for the mechanics.

categories.name: Authoring & content
categories.blurb: Write pages as plain text and let lazysite turn them into a site.

features.title: Markdown, served instantly
features.what: Drop a .md file in the docroot and it is served as a fully rendered HTML page on first request, then cached. No build step, no pipeline.

features.title: Template Toolkit variables
features.what: Pages can carry dynamic values  -  site config, page front matter, computed expressions, conditionals and loops  -  resolved at render time.

features.title: Reusable content includes
features.what: Inline shared Markdown partials, code files or remote fragments into a page so common content lives in one place.

features.title: Cached remote includes
features.what: Includes can fetch remote content and cache it for a set time, so external data appears inline without a fetch on every request.

features.title: Pull in remote pages
features.what: A .url file makes lazysite fetch Markdown from a remote URL (such as a GitHub raw file) and render it through the same pipeline  -  so docs can live with the code.

features.title: Rich layout blocks (fenced divs)
features.what: Wrap content in :::named blocks that become styled containers  -  callouts, columns, hero sections  -  without leaving Markdown.

features.title: Embed media (oEmbed)
features.what: Paste a video or social URL and lazysite expands it into the proper embed automatically.

features.title: List and scan pages
features.what: Generate indexes, galleries and menus by scanning a folder of pages and looping over them  -  with optional filtering by tag, date or path.

features.title: Site search
features.what: A built-in search index and results page let visitors find content without an external service.

features.title: News feeds
features.what: Publish RSS and Atom feeds generated from your pages, kept in sync as content changes.

features.title: Sitemaps & registries
features.what: Auto-maintained sitemap.xml and llms.txt registries so search engines and AI agents can discover every page.

features.title: Dynamic pages from query parameters
features.what: Read URL query parameters in a page to vary its output  -  simple dynamic pages with no application code.

features.title: JSON & API output
features.what: Serve a page as pure data with api mode, or as an unwrapped fragment with raw mode  -  the same content reused as an API.

features.title: Custom error pages
features.what: Author your own 404 (and other status) pages as ordinary Markdown.

categories.name: Design: layouts & themes
categories.blurb: Structure, style and content stay in separate layers that never touch.

features.title: Layouts and themes
features.what: A layout.tt owns the HTML chrome; a theme on top supplies colours, fonts and assets; content stays in .md files. Swap any layer independently.

features.title: Design tokens
features.what: A theme declares its palette and type as tokens in theme.json, auto-emitted as CSS custom properties  -  re-skin a site by editing values, not stylesheets.

features.title: Per-page layout override
features.what: Any single page can opt into a different layout from front matter  -  a landing page or print view without affecting the rest.

features.title: Install & publish themes
features.what: Install layouts and themes from a catalogue, or publish your own  -  including pulling them from a remote source.

features.title: Multiple views
features.what: Define alternate renderings of the same content for different audiences or formats.

categories.name: Dynamic & interactive
categories.blurb: Forms, accounts and payments are built in  -  no plugins to bolt on.

features.title: Customisable forms, built in
features.what: Build a contact, signup or feedback form right in Markdown with the :::form syntax - it works out of the box, validates input, and delivers to email or a handler. Fully customisable, and no third-party service or code required.

features.title: Authentication & access control
features.what: Protect pages or whole sections behind sign-in, with the right state revealed per visitor.

features.title: Payments (x402)
features.what: Gate content or downloads behind a payment using the x402 protocol, built into the core.

features.title: Navigation from a config file
features.what: The site menu is a plain nav.conf file read by the layout  -  edit the menu without touching templates.

features.title: Web manager UI
features.what: A browser-based manager to edit content, themes, navigation and users without the command line.

features.title: WebDAV & AI publishing
features.what: Publish over WebDAV and a control API  -  the path an AI agent or script uses to manage a site with no GUI in the loop.

categories.name: Operate & maintain
categories.blurb: Files on disk, fast by default, and trivial to back up or move.

features.title: No database
features.what: Files are the source of truth. Nothing to provision, dump or migrate  -  back up by copying the folder.

features.title: Fast by default
features.what: Dynamic only on the first request; plain cached HTML is served after that, with fine-grained control over caching.

features.title: Static export
features.what: Run in generation mode to produce a fully static site you can host anywhere  -  the same content, no server process.

features.title: Local dev server
features.what: A built-in server renders any Markdown tree locally with live preview  -  no install needed to start.

features.title: Link auditing
features.what: Scan the site for broken internal links before you publish.

features.title: Configuration in one file
features.what: Site-wide settings live in a single lazysite.conf, with overrides where you need them.

features.title: Version-control ready
features.what: Everything is a file, so the whole site  -  content, layout and theme  -  lives in a git repository.

categories.name: Access, permissions & governance
categories.blurb: Decide exactly who can do what, lock content down, and keep a record of every change.

features.title: Fine-grained capabilities
features.what: Per-account grants control exactly what each user or token can do  -  edit content, manage themes or layouts, change config, create sub-users  -  checked once, server-side, across every surface.

features.title: Per-file access control
features.what: Map any path to an owner with read and write lists (users or groups). Access only ever narrows, the owner is always allowed, and the same check binds the manager, WebDAV and the AI connector.

features.title: File locking
features.what: Lock a file so no one else can alter it. One lock store is shared across the manager and WebDAV, so a save anywhere respects a live lock.

features.title: Account & token expiry
features.what: Accounts and machine tokens can carry an expiry, after which access fails closed  -  useful for time-boxed contributors and partners.

features.title: Sub-users & delegation
features.what: A partner can mint scoped sub-accounts; onward delegation requires holding the capability itself, so nobody can grant more authority than they have.

features.title: Audit trail
features.what: An append-only log records who changed what, to what, when, from where, and the outcome  -  material changes only, never browsing.

features.title: Two-factor authentication
features.what: Optional TOTP second factor with recovery codes, self-contained with no external dependency.

features.title: Protected internals
features.what: Scripts, secrets and credential files can never be read or written through the content tools  -  a hard deny-list, enforced on reads as well as writes.

categories.name: AI & automation
categories.blurb: Built for a human/AI partnership  -  agents publish through the same rules a person does.

features.title: AI publishing over WebDAV & API
features.what: An agent or script edits content over WebDAV and a JSON control API  -  no GUI in the loop  -  bound by exactly the same capabilities and ACLs as any human author.

features.title: MCP AI connector
features.what: A built-in connector speaks the Model Context Protocol, so assistants such as Claude can manage a site through first-class tools.

features.title: OAuth for AI partners
features.what: Web-based AI connectors authenticate via a minimal OAuth 2.1 server with PKCE  -  the person proves they may act as a partner, and no secret is ever typed into the third party.

features.title: One enforced core
features.what: Every door  -  browser, API, WebDAV, MCP  -  translates to the same shared handlers, so a lock, an ACL or a capability holds identically whoever knocks.
